29.7: For the service of the house of God they contributed: five thousand talents and ten thousand gold coins of gold, ten thousand talents of silver, eighteen thousand talents of bronze, and a hundred thousand talents of iron.
29.8: Those who possessed precious stones gave them to the treasury of the house of the LORD, entrusted to Jehiel the Gershonite.
29.9: The people rejoiced because their leaders had given so willingly and wholeheartedly to the LORD. And King David was filled with great joy.
29.10: David blessed the LORD in the sight of the whole assembly. He said, 'Blessed are you, LORD, God of our father Israel, from everlasting to everlasting.'
29.11: 'Yours, LORD, is the greatness and the power and the glory and the victory and the majesty — everything in heaven and earth belongs to you. Yours, LORD, is the kingdom; you are exalted as head over all.'
29.12: 'Riches and honor come from you alone, and you rule over all. Strength and might are in your hand, and it is yours to make great and to give strength to all.'
29.13: 'Now therefore, our God, we give you thanks and praise your glorious name.'
29.14: 'But who am I, and who are my people, that we should be able to give so willingly? Everything comes from you, and we have given you only what came from your own hand.'
29.15: 'For we are strangers and sojourners before you, as all our ancestors were. Our days on earth pass like a shadow, and there is no permanence.'
29.16: 'O LORD our God, all this abundance we have gathered to build a house for your holy name — it all came from your hand. It is entirely yours.'
29.17: 'I also know, my God, that you examine the heart and delight in integrity. With an honest heart I have freely offered all these things. And now I have watched with joy as your people present here have given to you so willingly.'
29.18: 'LORD, God of Abraham, Isaac, and Israel, our fathers — keep this disposition forever in the hearts and minds of your people, and turn their hearts toward you.'
29.19: 'Give my son Solomon an undivided heart to keep your commandments, your decrees, and your statutes — to carry out everything needed and to build the palace for which I have made all these preparations.'
29.20: David told the whole assembly, 'Now bless the LORD your God.' The entire assembly blessed the LORD, the God of their ancestors, and bowed their heads, prostrating themselves before the LORD and before the king.
29.21: The next day they made offerings to the LORD: a thousand bulls, a thousand rams, and a thousand lambs as burnt offerings, along with their drink offerings, and abundant sacrifices for all Israel.
29.22: They ate and drank before the LORD that day with great joy. Then they made David's son Solomon king a second time, anointing him before the LORD as ruler, and Zadok as priest.
29.23: Solomon sat on the LORD's throne as king in place of his father David. He prospered, and all Israel obeyed him.
29.24: All the princes, all the warriors, and all of King David's other sons submitted themselves to King Solomon.
29.25: The LORD gave Solomon extraordinary greatness in the sight of all Israel, bestowing on him a royal majesty beyond anything any previous king of Israel had possessed.
29.26: Thus David son of Jesse reigned over all Israel.
29.27: He reigned over Israel for forty years: seven in Hebron and thirty-three in Jerusalem.
29.28: He died at a ripe old age, full of years, wealth, and honor. His son Solomon succeeded him as king.
29.29: The acts of King David, from first to last, are recorded in the records of Samuel the seer, in the records of Nathan the prophet, and in the records of Gad the seer.
29.30: This encompassed everything about his reign and his power, and the events that came upon him and upon Israel and upon all the surrounding kingdoms.

23.1: When David had grown old and had lived a full life, he made his son Solomon king over Israel.
23.2: He assembled all the leaders of Israel together with the priests and Levites.
23.3: The Levites were counted, those thirty years old and above; their total, counted individually, was thirty-eight thousand.
23.4: Of these, twenty-four thousand were assigned to oversee the work of the LORD's house, and six thousand were to serve as officials and judges.
23.5: Four thousand were gatekeepers, and four thousand praised the LORD with the instruments David had made for worship.
23.6: David organized them into divisions according to the three sons of Levi: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.
23.7: The Gershonite clans: Laadan and Shimei.
23.8: The sons of Laadan: Jehiel the head, Zetham, and Joel — three in all.
23.9: The sons of Shimei: Shelomith, Haziel, and Haran — three. These were the clan heads belonging to Laadan.
23.10: The sons of Shimei were Jahath, Zina, Jeush, and Beriah — four sons.
23.11: Jahath was the head and Zizah the second. But Jeush and Beriah did not have many sons, so they were counted together as a single family unit.
23.12: The sons of Kohath: Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel — four clans.
23.13: The sons of Amram were Aaron and Moses. Aaron was set apart to consecrate the most holy things — he and his sons in perpetuity — to burn incense before the LORD, to minister to him, and to pronounce blessing in his name forever.
23.14: As for Moses the man of God, his sons were counted among the tribe of Levi.
23.15: Moses had two sons: Gershom and Eliezer.
23.16: The head of Gershom's descendants was Shebuel.
23.17: Eliezer had only one son, Rehabiah, who was the head of his line. Eliezer had no other sons, but Rehabiah's descendants were very numerous.
23.18: The head of Izhar's line was Shelomith.
23.19: Hebron's sons: Jeriah the first, Amariah the second, Jahaziel the third, and Jekameam the fourth.
23.20: Uzziel's sons: Michah the first and Jesiah the second.
23.21: The sons of Merari: Mahli and Mushi. Mahli's sons: Eleazar and Kish.
23.22: Eleazar died without sons, leaving only daughters. Their cousins, the sons of Kish, married them.
23.23: Mushi's sons: Mahli, Eder, and Jeremoth — three sons.
23.24: These were the sons of Levi, organized by their ancestral houses — the heads of families counted individually — all who carried out the service of the LORD's house, from twenty years old and above.
23.25: David had said, 'The LORD God of Israel has given his people rest, and he lives in Jerusalem permanently.'
23.26: The Levites would no longer need to carry the tabernacle or any of its equipment.
23.27: It was by David's final ordinances that the Levites were counted from twenty years old and up.
23.28: Their responsibility was to assist the priests — the sons of Aaron — in the service of the LORD's house: in the courts, in the chambers, in the purification of all holy things, and in the work of the sanctuary.
23.29: This included the rows of bread and the fine flour for the grain offering — the unleavened cakes, baked goods, fried offerings — and all kinds of measuring and weighing.
23.30: They were also to stand every morning to give thanks and praise to the LORD, and again every evening.
23.31: And to present all burnt offerings to the LORD on the sabbaths, at the new moons, and at the appointed festivals — in the proper number and according to the prescribed order — as a regular and ongoing practice before the LORD.
23.32: They were also responsible for the care of the tent of meeting, the care of the holy place, and the supporting care of the Aaronic priests in the service of the LORD's house.

24.1: These are the divisions of Aaron's sons. Aaron had four sons: Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
24.2: Nadab and Abihu died before their father and left no sons, so Eleazar and Ithamar served as the priests.
24.3: David, together with Zadok from Eleazar's line and Ahimelech from Ithamar's line, assigned them according to their duties and service roles.
24.4: More family heads were found among Eleazar's descendants than among Ithamar's, so the assignment reflected this: sixteen family heads from Eleazar's line and eight from Ithamar's.
24.5: They were assigned by lot — both groups together — for the leaders of the sanctuary and the leaders of God's house were found in both Eleazar's and Ithamar's lines.
24.6: Shemaiah son of Nethaneel, a Levitical scribe, recorded the assignments before the king and the officials, Zadok the priest, Ahimelech son of Abiathar, and the heads of the priestly and Levitical families — alternating one draw from Eleazar's line, then one from Ithamar's.
24.7: The first lot fell to Jehoiarib, the second to Jedaiah,
24.8: the third to Harim, the fourth to Seorim,
24.9: the fifth to Malchijah, the sixth to Mijamin,
24.10: the seventh to Hakkoz, the eighth to Abijah,
24.11: the ninth to Jeshua, the tenth to Shecaniah,
24.12: the eleventh to Eliashib, the twelfth to Jakim,
24.13: the thirteenth to Huppah, the fourteenth to Jeshebeab,
24.14: the fifteenth to Bilgah, the sixteenth to Immer,
24.15: the seventeenth to Hezir, the eighteenth to Aphses,
24.16: the nineteenth to Pethahiah, the twentieth to Jehezekel,
24.17: the twenty-first to Jachin, the twenty-second to Gamul,
24.18: the twenty-third to Delaiah, the twenty-fourth to Maaziah.
24.19: These were the service assignments for entering the LORD's house according to their prescribed procedure — following their father Aaron's pattern, as the LORD God of Israel had commanded him.
24.20: Now for the remaining Levites: from Amram's line, Shubael; from Shubael's line, Jehdeiah.
24.21: From Rehabiah's line: Isshiah the head.
24.22: From the Izharite line: Shelomoth; from Shelomoth's line, Jahath.
24.23: Hebron's sons: Jeriah the first, Amariah the second, Jahaziel the third, Jekameam the fourth.
24.24: From Uzziel's line: Michah; from Michah's line, Shamir.
24.25: Michah's brother was Isshiah; Isshiah's son was Zechariah.
24.26: The sons of Merari: Mahli and Mushi. And from Jaaziah — another of Merari's descendants — his son Beno.
24.27: Jaaziah's descendants in the Merarite line: Beno, Shoham, Zaccur, and Ibri.
24.28: Mahli's son Eleazar had no sons.
24.30: Also Mushi's sons: Mahli, Eder, and Jerimoth. These were the Levites, organized by their ancestral families.
24.31: These Levites also cast lots alongside their priestly brothers the sons of Aaron, before King David, Zadok, Ahimelech, and the heads of the priestly and Levitical families — regardless of whether they were senior or junior.

25.1: David and the military commanders set apart for service the sons of Asaph, Heman, and Jeduthun, who were to prophesy with harps, lyres, and cymbals. The roster of those serving was:
25.2: From Asaph's line: Zaccur, Joseph, Nethaniah, and Asarelah — Asaph's sons, serving under Asaph's direction, who prophesied according to the king's command.
25.3: From Jeduthun's line: his sons Gedaliah, Zeri, Jeshaiah, Shimei, Hashabiah, and Mattithiah — six — under their father Jeduthun's direction, who prophesied with the harp, giving thanks and praising the LORD.
25.4: From Heman's line: his sons Bukkiah, Mattaniah, Uzziel, Shebuel, Jerimoth, Hananiah, Hanani, Eliathah, Giddalti, Romamtiezer, Joshbekashah, Mallothi, Hothir, and Mahazioth.
25.5: All these were sons of Heman, the king's seer — given according to God's word to exalt his horn. God had given Heman fourteen sons and three daughters.
25.6: All these sons served under their father's direction in music for the LORD's house, with cymbals, lyres, and harps, for the service of God's house — under the king's order given to Asaph, Jeduthun, and Heman.
25.7: Including their skilled fellow musicians who were trained in the LORD's songs, the total was 288.
25.8: They cast lots for their duty assignments, regardless of rank — whether great or small, teacher or student alike.
25.9: The first lot fell to Joseph from Asaph's family — he and his brothers and sons, twelve. The second fell to Gedaliah — he and his brothers and sons, twelve.
25.10: The third lot fell to Zaccur — his sons and brothers, twelve.
25.11: The fourth lot fell to Izri — his sons and brothers, twelve.
25.12: The fifth lot fell to Nethaniah — his sons and brothers, twelve.
25.13: The sixth lot fell to Bukkiah — his sons and brothers, twelve.
25.14: The seventh lot fell to Jesharelah — his sons and brothers, twelve.
25.15: The eighth lot fell to Jeshaiah — his sons and brothers, twelve.
25.16: The ninth lot fell to Mattaniah — his sons and brothers, twelve.
25.17: The tenth lot fell to Shimei — his sons and brothers, twelve.
25.18: The eleventh lot fell to Azareel — his sons and brothers, twelve.
25.19: The twelfth lot fell to Hashabiah — his sons and brothers, twelve.
25.20: The thirteenth lot fell to Shubael — his sons and brothers, twelve.
25.21: The fourteenth lot fell to Mattithiah — his sons and brothers, twelve.
25.22: The fifteenth lot fell to Jeremoth — his sons and brothers, twelve.
25.23: The sixteenth lot fell to Hananiah — his sons and brothers, twelve.
25.24: The seventeenth lot fell to Joshbekashah — his sons and brothers, twelve.
25.25: The eighteenth lot fell to Hanani — his sons and brothers, twelve.
25.26: The nineteenth lot fell to Mallothi — his sons and brothers, twelve.
25.27: The twentieth lot fell to Eliathah — his sons and brothers, twelve.
25.28: The twenty-first lot fell to Hothir — his sons and brothers, twelve.
25.29: The twenty-second lot fell to Giddalti — his sons and brothers, twelve.
25.30: The twenty-third lot fell to Mahazioth — his sons and brothers, twelve.
25.31: The twenty-fourth lot fell to Romamtiezer — his sons and brothers, twelve.

26.1: The divisions of the gatekeepers: from the Korhites, Meshelemiah son of Kore, from the line of Asaph.
26.2: Meshelemiah's sons: Zechariah the firstborn, Jediael the second, Zebadiah the third, Jathniel the fourth,
26.3: Elam the fifth, Jehohanan the sixth, Elioenai the seventh.
26.4: Also Obed-edom's sons: Shemaiah the firstborn, Jehozabad the second, Joah the third, Sacar the fourth, Nethanel the fifth,
26.5: Ammiel the sixth, Issachar the seventh, Peulthai the eighth — for God had blessed Obed-edom.
26.6: Shemaiah his son also had sons who led their father's household, for they were men of outstanding ability.
26.7: Shemaiah's sons: Othni, Rephael, Obed, and Elzabad; his kinsmen Elihu and Semachiah were also men of ability.
26.8: All these were of Obed-edom's line — they, their sons, and their kinsmen, sixty-two men of ability for service.
26.9: Meshelemiah had sons and kinsmen — eighteen men of ability.
26.10: Hosah of the Merarites had sons: Simri was the head, for though he was not the firstborn, his father had appointed him chief.
26.11: Hilkiah the second, Tebaliah the third, Zechariah the fourth. All the sons and kinsmen of Hosah: thirteen.
26.12: These gatekeeper divisions, along with their leaders, were assigned posts in the LORD's house, facing one another.
26.13: They cast lots for each gate, small and great alike, according to their ancestral families.
26.14: The east gate fell by lot to Shelemiah. His son Zechariah, a wise counselor, drew the north gate.
26.15: The south gate fell to Obed-edom, and the storehouse to his sons.
26.16: Shuppim and Hosah received the west gate, with the Shallecheth Gate at the ascending road — one guard post beside another.
26.17: Eastward: six Levites; northward: four per day; southward: four per day; at the storehouse: two at each end.
26.18: At Parbar on the west side: four at the roadway and two at Parbar itself.
26.19: These were the gatekeeper divisions — from the Korahite line and from the Merarite line.
26.20: Among the Levites, Ahijah had oversight of the treasuries of God's house and the treasuries of dedicated gifts.
26.21: From the sons of Ladan — the Gershonites belonging to Ladan — the heads of ancestral families were the Jehielites.
26.22: The sons of Jehiel — Zetham and his brother Joel — had oversight of the treasuries of the LORD's house.
26.23: Also from the Amramites, Izharites, Hebronites, and Uzzielites:
26.24: Shebuel son of Gershom, son of Moses, was the chief official over the treasuries.
26.25: His relatives through Eliezer: Rehabiah, then Jeshaiah, then Joram, then Zichri, then Shelomith.
26.26: This Shelomith and his kinsmen oversaw all the treasuries of dedicated gifts that King David, the family heads, the military commanders of thousands and hundreds, and the army officers had consecrated.
26.27: From battle spoils they had dedicated these gifts to sustain and repair the LORD's house.
26.28: All that Samuel the seer, Saul son of Kish, Abner son of Ner, and Joab son of Zeruiah had dedicated — whatever was a dedicated offering — was in the care of Shelomith and his kinsmen.
26.29: From the Izharites, Chenaniah and his sons handled the external affairs of Israel as officials and judges.
26.30: From the Hebronites, Hashabiah and his 1,700 capable kinsmen oversaw Israel on the west side of the Jordan for all matters relating to the LORD and the king's affairs.
26.31: Among the Hebronites, Jerijah was the leader, organized by the genealogies of their ancestral houses. In the fortieth year of David's reign, a search was made and mighty men of ability were found among them at Jazer in Gilead.
26.32: His kinsmen were 2,700 men of ability and family heads, whom King David appointed over the Reubenites, Gadites, and half tribe of Manasseh — for all matters concerning God and concerning the king.

27.1: These are the Israelites organized by number — the heads of families, commanders of thousands and hundreds, and their officers — who served the king by monthly rotation throughout the year, each division numbering twenty-four thousand.
27.2: Jashobeam son of Zabdiel commanded the first division for the first month, with twenty-four thousand men.
27.3: He was a descendant of Perez and was the senior commander for the first month.
27.4: Dodai the Ahohite commanded the second month's division, with Mikloth as his officer and twenty-four thousand men.
27.5: Benaiah son of Jehoiada the priest commanded the third month's division as chief, with twenty-four thousand men.
27.6: This was the Benaiah who was a mighty man among the thirty and above the thirty; his son Ammizabad was in charge of his division.
27.7: Asahel brother of Joab commanded the fourth month's division; after him his son Zebadiah. Their division: twenty-four thousand.
27.8: Shamhuth the Izrahite commanded the fifth month, with twenty-four thousand men.
27.9: Ira son of Ikkesh the Tekoite commanded the sixth month, with twenty-four thousand men.
27.10: Helez the Pelonite, from the tribe of Ephraim, commanded the seventh month, with twenty-four thousand men.
27.11: Sibbecai the Hushathite, of the Zerahite line, commanded the eighth month, with twenty-four thousand men.
27.12: Abiezer the Anetothite, from Benjamin, commanded the ninth month, with twenty-four thousand men.
27.13: Maharai the Netophathite, of the Zerahite line, commanded the tenth month, with twenty-four thousand men.
27.14: Benaiah the Pirathonite, from Ephraim, commanded the eleventh month, with twenty-four thousand men.
27.15: Heldai the Netophathite, descended from Othniel, commanded the twelfth month, with twenty-four thousand men.
27.16: The tribal officers: for Reuben, Eliezer son of Zichri; for Simeon, Shephatiah son of Maacah;
27.17: for the Levites, Hashabiah son of Kemuel; for the Aaronites, Zadok;
27.18: for Judah, Elihu, one of David's brothers; for Issachar, Omri son of Michael;
27.19: for Zebulun, Ishmaiah son of Obadiah; for Naphtali, Jerimoth son of Azriel;
27.20: for the sons of Ephraim, Hoshea son of Azaziah; for the half tribe of Manasseh, Joel son of Pedaiah;
27.21: for Manasseh's half tribe in Gilead, Iddo son of Zechariah; for Benjamin, Jaasiel son of Abner;
27.22: For Dan, Azareel son of Jeroham. These were the leaders of Israel's tribes.
27.23: David did not count those twenty years old and under, because the LORD had promised to multiply Israel like the stars of the heavens.
27.24: Joab son of Zeruiah had started counting but did not finish; wrath came upon Israel because of it, and the number was never recorded in the chronicles of King David.
27.25: Azmaveth son of Adiel managed the king's storehouses; Jonathan son of Uzziah managed the storehouses in the countryside — in the fields, cities, villages, and towers.
27.26: Ezri son of Chelub oversaw those who worked the royal fields.
27.27: Shimei the Ramathite oversaw the vineyards; Zabdi the Shiphmite managed the vintage and the wine cellars.
27.28: Baalhanan the Gederite managed the olive trees and sycamore trees in the lowlands; Joash managed the stores of oil.
27.29: Shitrai the Sharonite managed the herds grazing in Sharon; Shaphat son of Adlai managed the herds in the valleys.
27.30: Obil the Ishmaelite managed the camels; Jehdeiah the Meronothite managed the donkeys.
27.31: Jaziz the Hagerite managed the flocks. These were all the stewards of King David's royal holdings.
27.32: Jonathan, David's uncle, was a counselor — a man of discernment and a skilled scribe. Jehiel son of Hachmoni served the king's sons.
27.33: Ahithophel was the king's counselor; Hushai the Archite was the king's trusted companion.
27.34: After Ahithophel, Jehoiada son of Benaiah and Abiathar served as counselors. Joab was the commander of the king's army.

28.1: David assembled at Jerusalem all the leaders of Israel: the tribal princes, the commanders of the military divisions, the commanders of thousands and hundreds, the overseers of all royal property and livestock, and the court officials, the warriors, and all the men of valor.
28.2: King David stood up and said, 'Listen to me, my brothers and my people. I intended to build a house of rest for the ark of the covenant of the LORD — the footstool of our God — and I had made preparations for the construction.'
28.3: 'But God said to me, You are not to build a house for my name, for you are a man of war and have shed blood.'
28.4: 'Yet the LORD God of Israel chose me from all my father's house to be king over Israel permanently. He chose Judah as the leading tribe, then from Judah's house my father's family, and from my father's sons he was pleased to make me king over all Israel.'
28.5: 'And from all my sons — for the LORD has given me many — he has chosen Solomon to sit on the throne of the LORD's kingdom over Israel.'
28.6: 'God said to me, Solomon your son is the one who will build my house and my courts, for I have chosen him as my son and I will be his father.'
28.7: 'I will establish his kingdom forever, if he remains firm in keeping my commandments and decrees, as he has up to now.'
28.8: 'Now therefore, before all Israel — the LORD's assembly — and in the hearing of our God: keep and seek out all the commandments of the LORD your God, so that you may possess this good land and leave it as an inheritance to your children after you forever.'
28.9: 'And you, Solomon my son — know the God of your father. Serve him with a whole heart and a willing spirit, for the LORD searches every heart and understands every intention and thought. If you seek him, he will be found by you; but if you abandon him, he will reject you forever.'
28.10: 'Now take care — the LORD has chosen you to build a house as a sanctuary. Be strong and do it.'
28.11: David gave his son Solomon the plan for the temple portico, its buildings, storerooms, upper rooms, inner chambers, and the room for the mercy seat.
28.12: Also the plan for everything that had come to him by the Spirit: the courts of the LORD's house, all the surrounding chambers, the treasuries of God's house, and the treasuries of the dedicated offerings.
28.13: Also included were the divisions of priests and Levites, all the service work of the LORD's house, and specifications for all the vessels used in its service.
28.14: He specified the weight of gold for all the gold articles used in the various services, and the weight of silver for all the silver articles.
28.15: For each gold lampstand and its lamps: the exact weight of gold. For the silver lampstands and their lamps: the weight of silver required for each.
28.16: The weight of gold for each table of the bread of the Presence, and the weight of silver for the silver tables.
28.17: Pure gold for the forks, basins, and cups — and the exact weight for each golden bowl; the weight of silver for each silver bowl.
28.18: Refined gold by weight for the incense altar, and the plan for the golden chariot — the cherubim that spread their wings and covered the ark of the covenant of the LORD.
28.19: 'All this,' said David, 'came to me in writing from the LORD's hand — every detail of the plan.'
28.20: David said to his son Solomon, 'Be strong and courageous and do it. Do not be afraid or discouraged, for the LORD God — my God — is with you. He will not abandon you or forsake you until all the work for the LORD's house is complete.'
28.21: Look — the priestly and Levitical divisions for all the service of God's house are ready for you. With you for all the work will be every willing skilled craftsman for any service, and the officers and all the people are completely at your disposal.
